title: Engineering Trading Discipline into Code (Part 2): Building a Daily Trade Limit Enforcer
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: HIGH EXTRACT — 3-state machine (ALLOWED/CAUTION/LIMIT), amber threshold for early warning, automatic position closure on limit breach, 1-second cache refresh. Completes the discipline system from Part 64.

three_state_machine.CAUTION: trades >= (limit - amber) AND trades < limit — warning mode
three_state_machine.LIMIT: trades >= limit — halt all new trades
three_state_machine.transition: if trades >= limit: LIMIT; elif limit - trades <= amber: CAUTION; else: ALLOWED

enforcement_mechanisms.pending_order_cancellation: On LIMIT: iterate open orders, cancel all pending via TRADE_ACTION_REMOVE (market orders not touched)
enforcement_mechanisms.position_closure: OnTradeTransaction DEAL_ADD: if state==LIMIT and position just opened, immediately close with opposing market order
enforcement_mechanisms.existing_positions: Positions opened BEFORE limit was reached are NOT closed — only NEW positions after limit

get_day_start.logic: Parse configured start time (e.g. 09:00). If current time < today 09:00, use yesterday's date. Construct datetime anchor.
get_day_start.purpose: Consistent daily reset across broker timezones

global_variable_communication.DTL_LIMIT: Max trades per day (stored as double, used as int)
global_variable_communication.DTL_START_TIME: Trading day start as HHMM integer (900 = 09:00)
global_variable_communication.DTL_AMBER: Remaining trade threshold for CAUTION state
global_variable_communication.pattern: Dashboard writes; shared library + enforcer reads — single source of truth

caching.invalidation: On SetParameters() call (config change triggers refresh)

keep: Pending order cancellation on LIMIT (clean slate)
Position closure on new open after LIMIT (immediate reversal)
Global variable pattern for cross-component communication
discard: MQL5 global variables (use Python shared state / Redis)
improve: Implement in Python as DisciplineEngine.check_state() returning DisciplineState enum
CAUTION state: tighten TP targets (take profit faster when near limit)
CAUTION state: skip corrective zone trades (only take impulsive setups)
Persist daily state to file/DB for recovery after restart

creviaderiv_application.state_machine_in_python: Add DisciplineState to TradingOrchestrator; check before execute_signal()
creviaderiv_application.caution_behavior: In CAUTION: set enable_corrective_trades=False and MIN_RRR=2.0 (only take best setups)
creviaderiv_application.limit_behavior: In LIMIT: skip all new signals for the day; log reason
